items.title: After a U-turn and coming to a stop yesterday, the Qatari LNG tanker switched off her beacon. She has now re-started broadcasting her position, having...
items.description_html:
After a U-turn and coming to a stop yesterday, the Qatari LNG tanker switched off her beacon. She has now re-started broadcasting her position, having exited the Strait of Hormuz and nearing Pakistan.

It's the 2nd Qatari LNG tanker SoH crossing since the war started.


Javier Blas: Another Qatar-owned LNG tanker is nearing the mouth of the Strait of Hormuz. If she traverses, it would be the 2nd sucesfull export for Qatar since the war started. The Mihzem tanker is signalling Pakistan as destination. So far, she's heading via the Iranian shipping lane.

items.title: MAP OF THE DAY: Iran is claiming a much wider geographical footprint for the Strait of Hormuz than before, according to state media. Tehran says the S...
items.description_html:
MAP OF THE DAY: Iran is claiming a much wider geographical footprint for the Strait of Hormuz than before, according to state media.

Tehran says the SoH area goes from the port of Jask in the Gulf of Oman to the island of Siri in the Persian Gulf (both circled in red).

Effectively, it means Iran sees the SoH as an area about 200 kilometers to each side of strait's apex, significantly larger than in the past.

items.description_html:
President Trump tells CBS News he wants to “pause” the U.S. federal tax on gasoline (set at ~18 cents per gallons of gasoline and ~24 cents of diesel).

Republican Senator John Hawley says he’s introducing legislation today to fulfill the White House’s desire.


Nancy Cordes: In a phone interview, President Trump told me that he wants to pause the 18 cent federal gas tax “for a period of time.”

“I think it’s a great idea,” he said. “Yup, we’re going to take off the gas tax for a period of time, and when gas goes down, we’ll let it phase back in.”

Services imports increased 7.4% YoY, following a contraction in November (-2.04%), contributing to the net expansion.

items.title: In November, U.S. exports grew 6.1% YoY, slowing from 15.8% in October, while imports rose 6.5% month-on-month but stayed lower YoY. Exports to China ...
items.description_html:
In November, U.S. exports grew 6.1% YoY, slowing from 15.8% in October, while imports rose 6.5% month-on-month but stayed lower YoY.

Exports to China fell for the 11th consecutive month, with China’s share of U.S. exports dropping to 3.8%. Exports to Taiwan and India rose YoY.

items.title: December’s pickup followed a 7.2% expansion in November, a clear improvement over the 3.7% growth a year earlier. The acceleration was supported by s...
items.description_html: December’s pickup followed a 7.2% expansion in November, a clear improvement over the 3.7% growth a year earlier. The acceleration was supported by strength in manufacturing, mining, and electricity. Consumer durables and infrastructure goods also posted double-digit gains.

items.title: Semiconductor stocks have never been bigger: The Semiconductor Index, $SOX, now accounts for a record 23% of the S&P 500’s market cap. This percentag...
items.description_html:
Semiconductor stocks have never been bigger:

The Semiconductor Index, $SOX, now accounts for a record 23% of the S&P 500’s market cap.

This percentage has DOUBLED over the last 2 years.

This comes as the index has rallied +146% over this period, outperforming the S&P 500’s gain of +43%.

The index has also finished positive in 24 of the last 28 trading days, surging +65% over this period.

As a result, $SOX is now trading +60% above its 200-day moving average, the largest divergence since March 2000, when it was +110%.

Momentum in chip stocks is accelerating.

items.title: Market breadth is deteriorating: Only 22% of stocks in the S&P 500 have outperformed the index over the last 30 days, the 3rd-lowest reading since 199...
items.description_html:
Market breadth is deteriorating:

Only 22% of stocks in the S&P 500 have outperformed the index over the last 30 days, the 3rd-lowest reading since 1996.

This is down from 65% recorded in February, the highest percentage in 8 months and less than half of the long-term average of 50%.

Over the last 30 years, there have been only 2 brief periods when market breadth was weaker, in 2024 and 2025.

This comes as the Magnificent 7 now accounts for a near-record 35% of the S&P 500’s market cap, while the top 10% account for ~40%, also shy of an all-time high.

As a result, Information Technology and Communication Services sectors represent 46% of the S&P 500’s market value.

Big tech is all that matters.

items.title: The AI revolution is driving historic market momentum: The S&P 500 excluding AI names is up +16% over the last 2 years and is trading below its Februa...
items.description_html:
The AI revolution is driving historic market momentum:

The S&P 500 excluding AI names is up +16% over the last 2 years and is trading below its February all-time high.

Meanwhile, the S&P 500 as a whole is up +42% and has posted 15 all-time highs year-to-date.

The S&P 500 has now surged for 6 consecutive weeks, its longest winning streak since October 2024.

Over this period, the index is up +16%, while the S&P 500 equal-weighted index is up +8%.

This comes as 10 stocks have driven 69% of the S&P 500's gains during this time, with the remaining 490 representing just 31%.

Alphabet, $GOOGL, NVIDIA, $NVDA, and Amazon, $AMZN, alone accounted for 15%, 10%, and 8%, respectively.

The AI race is accelerating.

items.title: BREAKING: Anthropic's market-implied pre-IPO valuation surges to a record $1.4 trillion, rising another +40% in 24 days. This puts Anthropic's implied...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Anthropic's market-implied pre-IPO valuation surges to a record $1.4 trillion, rising another +40% in 24 days.

This puts Anthropic's implied valuation up +1,067% since October 2025, per onchain pre-IPO trading data.

Pre-IPO instruments trading onchain on Jupiter, backed 1:1 by SPV exposure, are providing a real-time proxy for the company’s implied IPO valuation.

The surge comes amid reports that Anthropic's annualized revenue has surged from $100 million in 2023 to $45 billion today.

Over the last 12 months alone, Anthropic's annualized revenue has risen +1,400%.

We are witnessing a historic technological revolution.

items.title: BREAKING: Just 25% of US non-homeowners expect to buy a home within the next 5 years, the lowest reading since Gallup first asked the question in 2013...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: Just 25% of US non-homeowners expect to buy a home within the next 5 years, the lowest reading since Gallup first asked the question in 2013.

This is down from 30% in 2025 and from 49% in 2017.

At the same time, 45% of non-homeowners say they do not expect to buy a home in the foreseeable future, up from 28% in 2013.

This shift is most pronounced among younger Americans, with only 29% of non-homeowners aged 18-34 expecting to buy within 5 years, down from 57% in 2013-2015.

US homeownership is increasingly out of reach.

items.title: BREAKING: OpenAI allowed more than 600 current and former employees to sell stock in October 2025, per WSJ. These employees collectively sold $6.6 bil...
items.description_html:
BREAKING: OpenAI allowed more than 600 current and former employees to sell stock in October 2025, per WSJ.

items.title: RT Maheshwer Peri: It isnt surprising that NEET exam has been cancelled by the @NTA_Exams . The hub of this entire operation is Sikar, where the succe...
items.description_html:
RT Maheshwer Peri
It isnt surprising that NEET exam has been cancelled by the @NTA_Exams . The hub of this entire operation is Sikar, where the success rate is 6X of national average. A similar thing happened in 2024 too, but the allegations were brushed under the carpet. Had we dealt with it in 2024 with an iron hand, this wouldnt have repeated.

What we know is that 140 of the 180 questions in NEET were part of a guess paper of 410 questions. What is alarming is that the order of the questions and the options were exactly the same.

The modus operandi is simple. In Sikar, the students are called in for a Mock test, a day before the actual exam and made to prepare for each of the questions in the guess paper. The students had 140 of the 180 questions preepared, thus guaranteed 600 of the 720 marks even before they entered the exam hall.

Time to go after the paper leakers. Jail them. 23 Lac students and families will pay the price of the brushing under the carpet we did in 2024. @careers360

items.title: Lots of countries announced measures early on to deal with the shortages/high prices due to #IranWar. India had earlier invoked the Essential Commodit...
items.description_html:
Lots of countries announced measures early on to deal with the shortages/high prices due to #IranWar.

India had earlier invoked the Essential Commodities Act to protect & control low LPG supply.

A (non-exhaustive) list of what others have done,

1. Philippines: Declared a state of national emergency due to the imminent danger of critically low energy supply.

2. Bangladesh: Daily fuel purchase limits by vehicle type. For eg - bikes up to 2 litres, pvt cars 10 litres etc. Shorter govt office hours. Early closure of markets & malls. And more.

3. S. Korea: Tightened public-sector vehicle rotation from five days to two days; vehicles restricted every alternate business day due to low fuel supply.

4. Indonesia: Announced fuel rationing; mandated civil servants to work from home once a week amid fuel supply disruptions and petrol-station shortages.

5. Australia: Sought agreements with Asian trade partners to secure refined fuel in return for LNG, as hundreds of service stations ran out of fuel.

6. Slovenia: Set a daily cap on road-fuel purchases at gas stations due to shortages at the pump.

#Nifty #GIFTNIFTY #BankNifty #India #IranWar‌ #Oil #Gold

items.title: RT Nimesh Shah: Macquarie on SBI O-P, TP Rs 1150 4Q FY26: Earnings miss driven by weak margins and trading losses NIM impacted by higher proportion of...
items.description_html:
RT Nimesh Shah
Macquarie on SBI
O-P, TP Rs 1150
4Q FY26: Earnings miss driven by weak margins and trading losses
NIM impacted by higher proportion of floating book
Asset quality remains a bright spot
Stock trades at 1.6x FY27E P/BV, which appears comfortable given expected ROEs of 15%.

JPM on SBI
OW, TP 1225
SBI reported a weaker than expected 4Q with NII (+3.8% y/y, -2% q/q) at Rs 443.8bn moderating vs +9% y/y in 3Q26 and coming 6% below JPM.
SBI recorded a 17bps q/q decline in NIM (whole bank) to 2.81% and an 18bps q/q decline in NIM (domestic) to 2.93%, vs. Street expectations of flattish to marginally lower margins q/q. However, SBI remains confident of NIM recovery in FY27 vs 4Q backed by multiple steps highlighted below.
Asset quality was benign with credit costs (on avg loans) at 24bps (-16bps q/q), although slippages (+3bps q/q) edged up slightly on seasonality.

MS on SBI
EW, TP Rs 980
Focus on call was on sharp NIM decline.
Management expects some recovery in NIM from 4Q level driven largely by loan yield recovery
Assume some recovery, yet cut F27- 28 NIM forecast by >20bp
Trim F27 & F28 EPS forecasts by 4% & 2%
Management guided to FY27 loan growth of 13-15%, assuming no material macro deterioration

CLSA on SBI
O-P TP Rs 1275
Reported a 4% beat on its 4QFY26 PBT driven by lower-than-expected opex and credit costs & a higher recovery from written-off accounts.
However, on top line, it was a weak quarter, with NII missing estimates by 5%.
This was driven by a 17bp sequential compression in NIM, sharper than expected
Calculated loan yields dropped 40bp to 7.5% driven by repo rate cuts, MCLR repricing & an increase in EBLR share.
Apart from NIM, it was a good quarter.
Loan growth of 17% YoY was best in class, while deposit growth picked up from 9% to 11% YoY
CASA ratio was steady YoY at 39%.
Slippages were steady at 0.5%, & credit costs were 0.2%.

BoFA Sec on SBI
Buy, TP Rs 1200
PAT growth of 6% YoY led by strong loan growth and lower credit cost offset by NIM compression and lower non‑interest income
NIM contraction led by repo‑linked loan repricing, MCLR resets post rate cuts, and mix shift towards T‑bill‑linked loans
FY27 guidance - 13-15% loan growth, full year NIM >3% , <50 bps credit cost and 1%+ RoA

Bernstein on SBI
Market Perform, TP Rs 1300
SBI delivered a mixed 4Q26
Loan growth accelerated to 17% YoY but NIM saw a sharp 18 bps QoQ decline to 2.93%, steepest compression amongst large banks.
Slippages rose sequentially, though largely seasonal, while headline GNPA improved to 1.49%.
RoA moderated 12 bps QoQ to 1.07%, weighed down by NIM compression & a Treasury MTM loss.
Management retained FY27 guidance of 13–15% credit growth, >3% domestic NIM & 50 bps credit costs
Management reaffirmed its through-cycle commitment to 1%+ RoA & 15%+ RoE

UBS on SBI
Neutral, TP Rs 1080
NII declined QoQ as margins dip 18bp QoQ due to loan pricing cut
Loan growth at c5% QoQ led by Corporates; asset quality stable
Management guides for loan growth of 13-15% for FY27; credit cost of c50bp
Expect bank's ROA to see downward pressure as treasury gains and credit costs normalize while margin trends remain subdued
Thus estimate ROA of 0.95% over FY27-28E (vs c1.12% in FY26) & believe risk reward at current valuations (1.2x FY27E P/BV) seems fair
Kotak Inst Eqt on SBI

Buy, TP Rs 1250
SBI reported about 5% yoy earnings growth, led by a 55% yoy decline in provisions, while operating profits declined about 10% yoy
Bank reported solid loan growth (~17% yoy), low credit costs (~25 bps) and a NIM decline of 20 bps qoq
Miss against expectations is likely to prompt a near-term pause in its re-rating, with investors awaiting evidence of recovery before rerating bank.
@CNBCTV18News @_RituSingh

items.title: I like to talk about stocks more , when they offer larger margin of safety to longer term averages , I don't like to talk about it when they are at 20...
items.description_html:
I like to talk about stocks more , when they offer larger margin of safety to longer term averages , I don't like to talk about it when they are at 20 times book and are clearly overvalued and the hot theme

Hence when I tweet about a company today for some it is boring , but 1 year later I see so much interest in the same stock , to stay ahead of the curve reading through companies that are good companies in tough times is very important and to maintain that discipline to chase good valuations and good growth , not peak margins and peak multiples

Entry valuations is one of the single most important controllable factor , use it to make the equation in your favor !

Profit pool delta is usually at the unseen parts of the value chain , hence it is far more rewarding to look deeper in the value chain profit pools

Probabilistic nature of outcomes , survival through optionality , role of prepared luck and being antifragile Must read all these and a few more , will start sharing what I read on a weekly basis if you all are interested !

items.title: Commodity costs were rising sharply, but Ather still improved margins. EBITDA moved from -23.3% to -2.5% over two years (~2080 bps YoY improvement), e...
items.description_html:
Commodity costs were rising sharply, but Ather still improved margins.

EBITDA moved from -23.3% to -2.5% over two years (~2080 bps YoY improvement), even as:
– Aluminium & metals prices rose ~41%
– Battery and electronics costs remained volatile

What helped:
• Better sourcing & inventory planning
• Use of alternate materials (light rare-earth magnets)
• Shift to LFP batteries
• Product design changes

A good example of how execution can offset cost pressures.

items.title: Urban Company🔻10% post results India Consumer Services excluding InstaHelp grew 26% YoY in Q4 to 808 Cr, which was the fastest growth in 11 quarter...
items.description_html:
Urban Company🔻10% post results

India Consumer Services excluding InstaHelp grew 26% YoY in Q4 to 808 Cr, which was the fastest growth in 11 quarters.

Adjusted EBITDA margin improved to 3.3% of NTV vs 1.6% last year

For FY26, core India business delivered 131 Cr of adjusted EBITDA at 4.1% of NTV

This is important because the business was loss making in FY24

International business is also scaling well
UAE & Singapore delivered 84% NTV growth in Q4 to 211 Cr.

For FY26, international NTV grew 75% to 700 Cr, business turned adjusted EBITDA positive at 6 Cr

Native is also moving in the right direction

NTV grew 67% YoY in Q4 to 89 Cr
For FY26, Native NTV stood at 345 Cr, growing 122% YoY

Adjusted EBITDA loss narrowed from 39 Cr in FY25 to 31 Cr in FY26

The only major drag is InstaHelp

InstaHelp did not exist at the start of FY26, but already delivered 2.7 Mn orders & 40 Cr NTV in Q4

March alone crossed 1.1 Mn orders
However, the business reported 119 Cr adjusted EBITDA loss in Q4

This loss is mainly due to customer acquisition, network density subsidies & supply onboarding

For FY26, the core business ex-InstaHelp generated 106 Cr adj EBITDA

Management has retained its target of cons. adj EBITDA breakeven by Q3FY28 & 1,000 Cr adj EBITDA by FY31 (Valuations are no way cheap)

InstaHelp is the only large investment drag today
At present, InstaHelp is losing Rs 441 per order (that is insane), but it is necessary because the company is aggressively investing to build the category

items.title: RT Nimit Vora, CFA: Okay here is the performance. This is the PMS wrapper. Has a 3 year history. Doesn’t beat the largest funds and the multicap inde...
items.description_html:
RT Nimit Vora, CFA
Okay here is the performance.

This is the PMS wrapper. Has a 3 year history.

Doesn’t beat the largest funds and the multicap index.

Returns are pre tax and post expenses. Do note the performance fees would not have accrued in the last 2 years due to flat markets.


Dhirendra Kumar: @sandeepjethwani The pitch reads well. The math underneath does not.
The PMS fee sits on top of the mutual fund expense ratio, not in place of it. All-in: 1.1 to 2%. A direct MF portfolio does the same job at 0.5 to 1%.

Terence Tao, aged just 13, got 1/7 on that problem, aced almost everything else, and still won gold.

items.title: RT DepressedBergman: Satyajit Ray on how audience from other countries can connect with his movies: "There are obvious risks in exposing an audience t...
items.description_html:
RT DepressedBergman
Satyajit Ray on how audience from other countries can connect with his movies:

"There are obvious risks in exposing an audience to a wholly alien culture. There was an American lady, for instance, who was so upset by the spectacle of Indians eating with their fingers that she had to leave the theatre as soon as the second dinner episode commenced. But such squeamishness is not common. If details confuse, or offend, or go over the head, there is always the human element to fall back upon.

In 'Apur Sansar' (1959), Apu attends the wedding of a friend’s sister. The bridegroom (this is an arranged marriage) arrives and is found to be touched in the head. With the bride’s mother refusing to give away her daughter, the marriage seems on the verge of being abandoned. If it doesn’t take place at the appointed hour, the bride would be cursed and made unmarriageable for life. The crisis is averted at the last moment by Apu offering to step in as the groom. A Western viewer ignorant of orthodox Hindu customs must find the episode highly bizarre. But since Apu himself finds it so, and since his action is prompted by compassion, the viewer accepts it on moral grounds, though given no opportunity to weigh the pros and cons of a seemingly irrational practice.

Faced with a film from an alien culture, swamped by details which evoke no responses in him, a viewer will often find that the only aspect which offers a common ground is the moral one. Broad concepts of good and evil, right and wrong, do not vary much from culture to culture. Human behaviour also falls within patterns which are largely familiar and predictable. Abnormal situations may affect such behaviour as, say, in Hitler’s Germany; but as long as a film makes the context clear, a viewer is unlikely to lose his bearings. This can happen only when the development of the story itself turns on social and historical factors of which the viewer has no knowledge."

("Deep Focus- Reflections on Cinema", Satyajit Ray, 2011)

P.S: On this day, 67 years ago, 'Apur Sansar' was released in India.

items.title: RT Tijori: The company is Ajinomoto Co. (TYO: 2802), and the product that matters here is Ajinomoto Build-up Film, or ABF. What makes this interesting...
items.description_html:
RT Tijori
The company is Ajinomoto Co. (TYO: 2802), and the product that matters here is Ajinomoto Build-up Film, or ABF.

What makes this interesting is the switching-cost moat. ABF is a thermosetting film that has to maintain sub-micron flatness across a 600mm sheet, and every single layer needs to be defect-free because one flaw means you scrap the entire 20-layer substrate carrying a $40,000 GPU die. Ajinomoto spent three decades dialing in the exact resin-filler chemistry, controls both the IP and the precursor amino-acid feedstock, and qualifying a new film with TSMC, Intel, or Samsung takes five to seven years of joint testing. So even if Sekisui or a Chinese competitor cracked the chemistry tomorrow, no chipmaker is going to risk a yield disaster just to switch suppliers.

The cleaner play here isn't actually Ajinomoto itself — the food business still accounts for roughly 70% of revenue, which dilutes the AI thesis and is exactly why Palliser has been pushing for a spin-off. If you want pure-play exposure, look downstream at the ABF substrate manufacturers: Unimicron, Nan Ya PCB, Kinsus, Ibiden, Shinko Electric, and AT&S.


SemiAnalysis: There is a single thin-film material required by every AI chip on earth. GPUs, TPUs, custom ASICs. All of them. 98% of global supply controlled by one Japanese chemical company. Zero production-ready alternatives. One producer fully booked through 2027. Raising prices. Lead times

items.title: DIXON delivered strong FY26 with 25.76% revenue growth to ₹4,887 Cr and robust cash generation (FCF ₹715 Cr, CFO/PAT 1.08x). However, Q4 saw a 35.9%...
items.description_html:
DIXON delivered strong FY26 with 25.76% revenue growth to ₹4,887 Cr and robust cash generation (FCF ₹715 Cr, CFO/PAT 1.08x). However, Q4 saw a 35.9% PAT decline due to the absence of prior year's exceptional gain, and operating margin compressed 124 bps missing expansion guidance.

The company's heavy capex cycle (₹1,068 Cr) and backward integration through Q Tech acquisition position it for future margin improvement, but near-term execution remains mixed with PLI scheme uncertainty pending.

Positive Factors

FY26 revenue grew 25.76% YoY to ₹4,887 Cr, exceeding management's strong growth target

Operating cash flow of ₹1,782 Cr with CFO/PAT ratio of 1.08x indicates high earnings quality

Free cash flow of ₹715 Cr positive despite heavy capex, up 240% YoY

Capex of ₹1,068 Cr delivered capacity expansion at Noida, Tirupati, Chennai and Q Tech acquisition

Company EBITDA margin expanded 131 bps to 5.25% including Other Income

Working capital remained negative at -7 days, a competitive advantage

Finance costs declined 10.94% YoY despite borrowings increasing 131% to ₹468 Cr

Risk Factors

Q4 PAT declined 35.9% YoY to ₹298 Cr due to absence of exceptional gain in Q4 FY25

Operating EBITDA margin compressed 124 bps YoY to 3.86%, missing guidance of 120-130 bps expansion

PLI income of ₹1,110 Cr recognized but pending disbursement; scheme expires March 2026

Depreciation grew 39.77% YoY, significantly outpacing revenue growth due to aggressive capex

NCI contribution increased to 12.5% of PAT from 11.1%, diluting owner earnings

Revenue growth decelerated to 2.1% YoY in Q4 from strong earlier quarters

items.title: Berger Paints delivered a strong Q4 with EBITDA margin of 18.3% (standalone) exceeding management's 15-17% guidance range and volume growth of 11.8% s...
items.description_html:
Berger Paints delivered a strong Q4 with EBITDA margin of 18.3% (standalone) exceeding management's 15-17% guidance range and volume growth of 11.8% surpassing the ~10% target. PAT grew 27.5% YoY demonstrating strong operating leverage despite raw material cost pressures.

The 10-quarter high gross margin of 42.3% and accelerating volume growth (11.8% vs 8% in Q3) indicate a sustained margin inflection. Price increases of over 11% from March 2026 and strong demand recovery in urban markets provide near-term catalysts. The debt-free balance sheet and strong cash conversion (1.28x) support continued execution.

Positive Factors

Q4 Standalone EBITDA margin 18.3% exceeded 15-17% guidance range by 130-330 bps

Q4 volume growth 11.8% exceeded management guidance of ~10% by 180 bps

PAT grew 27.5% YoY to ₹335.25 crore, significantly outpacing revenue growth of 6.1%

Gross margin at 42.3% is a 10-quarter high, indicating sustained margin improvement

Operating cash flow of ₹1,406.43 crore represents 1.28x cash conversion quality

Company maintains debt-free balance sheet with Debt/Equity at 0.02x

Volume growth accelerated from 8% in Q3 to 11.8% in Q4 showing demand momentum

Subsidiaries and JVs delivered double-digit growth in topline and operating profit

Risk Factors

Raw material costs grew 7.7% YoY vs revenue growth 6.1%, indicating input price pressure

Total expenses grew 8.4% YoY vs revenue 6.1%, causing margin compression at operating level

Inventory build of ₹180.83 crore in Q4 provides temporary margin boost but may reverse

FY26 PAT declined 4.6% YoY to ₹1,128.02 crore despite Q4 strength

Subsidiary contribution to PAT is modest at 2.4%, limiting diversification benefit

Nepal operations impacted by political instability affecting regional performance

items.title: KPR Mill delivered steady Q4 with PAT growth of 11.1% YoY driven by margin expansion in sugar segment (310 bps) and strong cash generation. However, f...
items.description_html:
KPR Mill delivered steady Q4 with PAT growth of 11.1% YoY driven by margin expansion in sugar segment (310 bps) and strong cash generation. However, full-year EBITDA margin contracted 45 bps and textile segment faces margin pressure.

The company maintains a strong balance sheet with net cash of ₹772 Cr and is investing heavily in capex (₹315 Cr, +87% YoY) for modernization. Without explicit management guidance, the surprise magnitude is none - performance is solid but not exceptional.

Positive Factors

PAT grew 11.1% YoY to ₹227.17 Cr, outpacing revenue growth of 0.9%

Q4 EBITDA margin expanded 71 bps YoY to 19.52%

Sugar segment margin expanded 310 bps YoY to 15.5% with 36.7% profit growth

Operating cash flow at 1.28x PAT ratio indicating high-quality earnings

Net cash position of ₹772.17 Cr with debt/equity at
0.10x

Capex of ₹314.82 Cr (+87% YoY) driving modernization and capacity expansion

Subsidiaries contribute 30% of consolidated PAT - significant value creation

Risk Factors

Textile segment margin compressed 30 bps YoY to 16.6% despite 1.3% revenue growth

Raw material costs grew 6.4% YoY faster than revenue 0.9%

Employee costs grew 8.9% YoY outpacing revenue growth

Working capital used ₹37.89 Cr with receivables up ₹58.90 Cr

Operating cash flow declined 21% YoY to ₹1,107.84 Cr

FY26 EBITDA margin contracted 45 bps to 19.06%
from 19.51% in FY25

items.title: MobiKwik achieved second consecutive profitable quarter with PAT of ₹43.84 Mn, delivering on management's Q3/Q4 break-even guidance. EBITDA margin ex...
items.description_html:
MobiKwik achieved second consecutive profitable quarter with PAT of ₹43.84 Mn, delivering on management's Q3/Q4 break-even guidance. EBITDA margin expanded 2,312 bps YoY to 6.03% driven by 14.1% expense reduction.

However, full-year FY26 loss of ₹621 Mn and negative operating cash flow of ₹781 Mn highlight ongoing challenges. New NBFC and broking licenses secured provide future growth drivers, while 34% IPO proceeds remain unutilized.

Positive Factors

Second consecutive profitable quarter achieved with PAT of ₹43.84 Mn vs loss of ₹560.37 Mn in Q4 FY25

EBITDA margin expanded to 6.03% from -17.09% YoY — a 2,312 bps improvement

Revenue grew 7.8% YoY to ₹2,887.12 Mn while expenses declined 14.1% YoY

Lending operational expenses reduced 95% YoY

reflecting successful pivot to lower-risk ZIP EMI

New regulatory licenses secured: NBFC (April 2026)
and stock broking (February 2026)

Finance costs declined 23.4% YoY indicating reduced borrowing levels

₹1,821.55 Mn (34%) of IPO proceeds remain unutilized providing investment runway

Risk Factors

FY26 full-year loss of ₹621.01 Mn despite quarterly profitability — annual guidance not fully met

Fixed costs at ₹1,273.05 Mn exceeded management target of ₹1,050-1,100 Mn by 15-21%

Working capital deteriorated with trade receivables up 30% YoY

Operating cash flow negative at -₹781.40 Mn for FY26

Subsidiaries dragged consolidated PAT by ₹79.47 Mn (12.8% of loss)

Fraud incident of ₹403.59 Mn — ₹118.31 Mn remains unrecovered

items.title: Textiles Industry - Investment Trends • Textile industry saw a big jump in new investments in 2025-26, with 137 new projects worth about ₹56,000+ cr...
items.description_html:
Textiles Industry - Investment Trends

• Textile industry saw a big jump in new investments in 2025-26, with 137 new projects worth about ₹56,000+ crore announced.

• Total pending/outstanding textile projects increased sharply to 313, showing a huge future expansion pipeline in the sector.

• Around 105 projects are currently under construction/implementation, worth nearly ₹54,000+ crore.

• Only 3 old stalled projects were revived, so revival activity is still small compared to new investments.

• Compared to previous years, 2025-26 shows the strongest investment momentum in textiles, indicating companies are becoming more aggressive on expansion.

items.title: Global Copper Supply-Demand Model Copper Supply and Demand Outlook The copper market is expected to enter a period of growing deficits due to signific...
items.description_html:
Global Copper Supply-Demand Model

Copper Supply and Demand Outlook

The copper market is expected to enter a period of growing deficits due to significant supply constraints and increasing global demand.

Jefferies' supply and demand model forecasts that total global copper demand will grow at a CAGR of 2.1% from 2025 to 2030E, reaching 30,931 kt by 2030, while total supply is projected to grow at a slower CAGR of 1.7%, reaching 30,136 kt in the same period.

This imbalance is expected to result in a widening deficit, with a notional deficit of (795 kt) projected by 2030E.

items.title: Urban Company has stepped up incentives for both consumers and home service partners in its InstaHelp business during the January–March quarter - the...
items.description_html:
Urban Company has stepped up incentives for both consumers and home service partners in its InstaHelp business during the January–March quarter - the biggest contributor to its losses.

Loss per order increased by 17% to Rs 447, even as net order value declined.

Total losses from InstaHelp nearly doubled to Rs 119 crore, while orders grew 66% to 2.7 million in the January–March quarter compared to October–December.

It will be interesting to see how retention metrics evolve in this category. Some stats for InstaHelp:

- Monthly user retention is 30–33%, with users placing around 2.5 orders per month

- The top 10% of monthly users placed around 8 orders, while the top 1% placed around 21 orders in March 2026

items.title: RT Chad P. Bown: US energy exports to China have cratered under Trump 2.0. Crude, LNG, and even coal have plummeted since the beginning of 2025. (Thes...
items.description_html:
RT Chad P. Bown
US energy exports to China have cratered under Trump 2.0. Crude, LNG, and even coal have plummeted since the beginning of 2025. (These data are through March 2026.)

Even if there is a political announcement, why Chinese energy purchases will be nothing more than a distraction coming out of this week's Trump-Xi meeting.


Chad P. Bown: Unlike their 2020 phase one agreement, I am skeptical Chinese energy purchases will be a *meaningful* deliverable from Trump-Xi 2.0 for three reasons

- Why would China increase its vulnerabilities, becoming dependent on a rival for energy
- Trump can't commit to delivering
